title: Review-cadence discipline has a generation contract + post-hoc check but no per-spec in-execution self-check
description: The quarterly review-cadence discipline (B1/TASK-031: >=4 evaluate-and-adjust checkpoints, earliest before 1 Dec) has a generation contract (snippet 14) AND a post-hoc check (check_draft.check_review_cadence, Check 5) -- but NO per-spec in-execution self-check at propose-review-loop. Per DEC-54's standing rule (every transposed discipline = generation contract + in-execution check; post-hoc alone insufficient), this is a check-only structural discipline. Coverage (T3) + distribution (T4) got in-execution gates; cadence is the remaining structural discipline without one.

evidence.reference: check_review_cadence (~:598-633) is the post-hoc gate (>=4 checkpoints, earliest < 1 Dec), report-only by default / --strict to gate
frontmatter has no success_criteria cadence entry -> no per-spec in-execution self-check

impact: A review loop with <4 checkpoints or a back-loaded first checkpoint surfaces only at the post-hoc check (or final assembly), not during the propose-review-loop generation -- the FGAP-033 late-discovery class for the cadence discipline.
proposed_resolution: CLOSED by TASK-045 / VER-040 / commit be1bbf2: review-cadence now has a per-spec in-execution self-check (self_check.py review_cadence evaluator, reusing check_draft.check_review_cadence; structural fail -> apply exit 3) alongside its existing generation contract (snippet 14) + whole-draft check (Check 5). cycle_year sourced from grounding['cycle']['starts_on']; the cycle-unavailable case structural-fails ('cycle year unavailable') rather than degrading. Workshop-only (no production touch); snippet-14 frontmatter-only change (parity 14/14). [Original plan: register a cadence self-check evaluator reusing check_review_cadence + a snippet-14 structural success_criteria, mirroring the T3 coverage / T4 distribution pattern.]
